content: Hey, I'm trying to optimize my morning routine. I'm always rushing to get to work on time.
What time do you usually wake up, and what time do you need to be at work?
I set my alarm for 6:30, but I usually hit snooze until 6:45. I need to be at the high school by 7:45.
That gives you about an hour. What does your current routine look like?
I get up, make coffee, check my phone for 15 minutes, shower, get dressed, grab a granola bar, and head out. I live in Austin, so traffic can be unpredictable.
Have you considered prepping things the night before? Like laying out clothes or packing lunch?
I've tried that. I usually end up changing my mind about what to wear. I do keep my work bag packed though - got tired of forgetting my lesson plans.
What about breakfast? You mentioned a granola bar - would you prefer something more substantial if you had time?
Honestly, I'd love to make eggs sometimes, but I never have the time. I usually eat at my desk during my first prep period around 8:30.
What time do you typically go to bed? That might be affecting your morning energy levels.
I try to be in bed by 11, but I'm usually up until midnight scrolling through Reddit or watching YouTube videos about 3D printing.
Ah, that might explain the snooze button habit! What if you moved your phone charger across the room so you have to get up to turn off the alarm?
I've thought about it, but my apartment is small and I'd probably just go back to bed. Plus, I share the place with two roommates who stay up later than me.
Do you have any morning rituals that help you start the day right? Even small things can make a difference.
I do step outside for a minute to check the weather - helps me decide if I need a jacket for the walk to my car. And I always make that first cup of coffee, even if it's just to go.
